Agios Panteleimon Greek Orthodox Church

Agios Panteleimon the Healer

Our parish feast day falls on 26 July each year, celebrating the life and intercession of Agios Panteleimon the Great Martyr and Unmercenary Healer.

Our Parish Celebrates

Feast Day Through the Years

Photos from past celebrations of Agios Panteleimon's feast day at our parish.

The Bishop is welcomed during the entrance procession of the Liturgy.
Fr Kyriakos offers prayers before the gathered faithful.
The congregation faces the iconostasis during the Divine Liturgy.
A full church for the feast day Liturgy.
Antidoron prepared for the congregation during the service.
The blessing of the artoklasia bread, brought forward by the parish.
The Bishop blesses the artoklasia bread with the parish clergy.
The icon of Agios Panteleimon, adorned with flowers for his feast.
The Bishop, clergy and parishioners pause for a photograph before the iconostasis.
A priest offers a blessing to a parishioner outside the church.
Lighting candles together ahead of the celebration.
Overflow seating in the marquee, following the service on screen.
The marquee filled with parishioners for the feast day.
Candles lit in the marquee during the celebration.
Parishioners gather outdoors for fellowship after the service.
Two parishioners at the feast day celebration.
Loukoumades and refreshments laid out for the parish.
Sharing loukoumades after the Liturgy.
Parishioners gathered outside following the service.

Feast Day Schedule

Confirmed services for this year's celebration of our patron saint, as announced by the Diocese of Canberra.

Sunday 26 July
Orthros and Divine Liturgy
8:30 AM
Sunday 26 July
Great Vespers
5:30 PM
Monday 27 July
Matins and Divine Liturgy
8:00 AM
Sunday 2 August
Feast Day Divine Liturgy
8:00 AM

We look forward to welcoming all the faithful to Goulburn. Our parish raffle will be drawn on Sunday, 2 August, after the festivities.
